item.title: Six U.S. federal agencies face a July 18 deadline to finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin rules on capital, reserves, and AML compliance.
SEC placed three crypto rulemaking items on its July agenda covering digital asset sales, broker-dealer custody, and market structure.
Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock, Coinbase, and Stripe joined 140 firms to back Open USD (OUSD), a new stablecoin launching natively on Solana.
U.S. Senate crypto market structure bill faces floor vote delay as Democrats demand conflict-of-interest provisions before August recess.
EU MiCA framework entered full enforcement July 1; crypto firms without CASP authorization must halt operations across EU member states.
Trump said Bitcoin used as payment should not be subject to capital gains tax, aligning with U.S. pro-crypto legislation.
SEC plans to propose a rule in July 2026 exempting certain crypto activities from securities registration requirements.
U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $197M in net inflows for the week ending July 10, snapping an eight-week, $2B+ outflow streak.
U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs shed approximately $430M on July 13, with Fidelity FBTC losing $246M and BlackRock IBIT shedding $186M.
Circle shares fell approximately 8% after Stripe, Coinbase, and BlackRock backed Open USD (OUSD), positioning it as a rival to USDC.
FDIC confirmed stablecoin token holders will not receive federal deposit insurance under the GENIUS Act framework.
EU MiCA Transfer of Funds Regulation requires crypto service providers to collect full originator and beneficiary data on every transfer.
SEC is preparing a public comment process on new spot crypto ETF categories including prediction markets and leveraged crypto products.
Polychain and Variant-backed perpetuals platform Cascade lost $1.34M in USDC on July 16 in an exploit on its pre-launch CLS vault.
Security researchers disclosed a patched Aptos blockchain flaw that could have exposed $70B in assets, exploitable using a $3,000 server.
